Badger Grapple Red — v20.4 Repainted Field House + Campus Quad

Built directly, no external art tool — I don't have a generative image AI
(no DALL-E/Midjourney access in this environment), so this is procedural
pixel art: real shading algorithms written in Python/PIL implementing the
specific techniques identified from the FireRed reference screenshots
(multi-tone gradient fills, dithered tone transitions, directional grass
texture, wood grain, cylindrical shading on rounded objects like the
punching bag). Being upfront: this is a real, meaningful step toward that
bar using an honest technique, not a claim of matching hand/AI-illustrated
art parity in one pass.

IMPORTANT CORRECTION FIRST: the previous art brief flagged
`assets/tiles/fieldhouse_tiles.png` as the highest-priority file to
repaint. That was wrong — I checked, and that file is loaded but never
actually drawn anywhere in the current engine (the overworld renders
entirely from the 7 painted `area_*.png` scene files, not tile assembly).
Repainting it would have been wasted effort. Redirected to the files that
are actually rendered.

WHAT CHANGED:
- assets/ui/area_fieldhouse.png: repainted. Wood floor with grain and
  plank seams, dithered padded walls, a real gradient-shaded wrestling
  mat, shaded 4-locker bank, coach desk with props, potted plants with
  layered leaf clusters, a punching bag with cylindrical shading, a
  weight machine, shop counter, recovery table, meeting table+bench,
  weight-room floor mats, and two banners with actual readable text
  ("ON WISCONSIN" / "WORK HARD" — previously blank).
- assets/ui/area_campus.png: repainted. Multi-tone shaded lawn, a
  tree-lined perimeter, a real two-story building facade with windows
  and a door (previously nothing there), a hedge block, a statue, lamp
  posts, and a stone path connecting all 4 exits.

CAUGHT AND FIXED BEFORE SHIPPING: my first pass drew the Field House mat
at the wrong size/position — visually it spanned way outside the actual
walkable "M" interaction zone the game logic uses (checked precisely
against the isBlocked/spotKind coordinates in maps.js). Fixed to align
exactly with the real zone before shipping, so the visual mat now matches
where the game actually treats you as "on the mat."

VERIFICATION (learned from a mistake last round where I screenshotted
the wrong scene and shipped un-reviewed art): both scenes were rendered
in a real headless browser, reached via actual button clicks replaying
the real boot-to-overworld flow (not synthetic keyboard events, which
silently failed to reach the canvas last time), confirmed zero console
errors, and visually reviewed before packaging.

Every element was placed against the exact pixel coordinates of its
real collision/interaction zone from src/data/maps.js — no decoration
was placed on a walkable tile where it would look solid but not
actually block movement.

Cache: ?v=204. Proof: title/note reads "v20.4 Repainted Field House + Campus".
